items.title: Toronto Maple Leafs fire general manager Brad Treliving
items.summary_auto: Maple Leaf Sports and Entertainment have decided to part ways with Toronto Maple Leafs General Manager Brad Treliving.

items.title: The National | Trump threatens to 'obliterate' Iran's infrastructure
items.summary_auto: March 30, 2026 | U.S. President Donald Trump threatens to 'completely obliterate' Iran's power plants, oil infrastructure and even desalination plants, unless a deal is reached. Air Canada's embattled CEO announces his retirement. And legendary Canadian pop star Céline Dion announces her big comeback. Watch The National 9 pm EST Sun - Fri

items.title: Alberta pushing for constitutional change to give provinces say over appointment of judges
items.summary_auto: Alberta's government is calling on Ottawa to change the Constitution to give provinces more of a say in how judges are appointed at the provincial level.

items.title: B.C. youth charged after chats with U.S. teen who allegedly made school shooting threats: police
items.summary_auto: A Sooke, B.C., youth has been arrested and charged after online chats with a U.S. teenager who allegedly made school shooting threats, RCMP say.

items.title: How provincial budget deficits impact you
items.summary_auto: Every province that has released a 2026-27 budget is projecting a deficit. But is that cause for concern? Note: P.E.I and Newfoundland and Labrador had not yet released their budgets at the time of publication.

items.title: Alberta NDP not considering name change — for now — amid distance from federal party
items.summary_auto: Despite speedy efforts to distance provincial New Democrats from the new-look federal party, Albertans shouldn't necessarily expect any changes to the provincial orange brand. But Alberta NDP Leader Naheed Nenshi told reporters Monday that if members of the party want to have a conversation around a name change, he's open to the possibility.

items.title: 40 species, including snowy owl, added to UN list for greater protections
items.summary_auto: A week of negotiations has led to 40 species added on a list of protections under the Convention on the Conservation of Migratory Species of Wild Animals, the 15th meeting of countries that are party to a UN treaty signed in 1979.

items.title: Man who crashed truck into Michigan synagogue inspired by Hezbollah, FBI says
items.summary_auto: An armed man who crashed his pickup truck into a major Detroit-area synagogue earlier in March was inspired by the Iran-backed militant group Hezbollah and had sought to inflict as much damage as he could on Jewish people, the FBI said Monday.

items.title: Former investor says he's disappointed by Moose Jaw scientist Goodenowe's 'new lies and new promises'
items.summary_auto: As Dayan Goodenowe makes new claims about a potential treatment for Alzheimer's, a former investor says the controversial Moose Jaw-based scientist has a history of making unproven promises that he can't keep.

items.title: Human rights group says at least 70 killed in wake of attack in Haiti's Artibonite region
items.summary_auto: At least 70 people were killed and 30 injured during an attack near Petite-Rivière in Haiti's Artibonite region, a human rights ‌group said on Monday, numbers that were significantly higher than official estimates which put the death toll at around 16.

items.title: New bill would remove deadlines for Alberta government to act on citizen petitions
items.summary_auto: If Bill 23 passes, it would be the third time the United Conservative government has substantively amended the Citizen Initiative Act, and the second substantive set amendments to recall legislation since those two democratic options were introduced in 2021.

items.title: Bread price-fixing settlement scammers are trying to get your dough
Human rights tribunal approves $8.5B child welfare deal for Ontario First Nations
items.summary_auto: The Canadian Human Rights Tribunal has approved a landmark First Nations child welfare deal between the federal government and First Nations in Ontario, partially ending a decades-long discrimination case.

items.title: Robert Land Academy, now-shut private school in Ontario, under police investigation for alleged child abuse
items.summary_auto: Peel Regional Police are leading an investigation into allegations of child abuse at Robert Land Academy, a military-style private school for boys in Ontario's Niagara Region that operated for decades until last year. Dozens of former students have alleged physical and emotional abuse, and in some cases, sexual exploitation.

items.title: Why Kharg Island is key for the U.S., Iran and the world's economy
items.summary_auto: Kharg Island is Iran's key oil export hub and perhaps the most important location in the war that the U.S. and Israel launched on Iran on Feb. 28.

items.title: Can Avi Lewis make the federal NDP relevant again — without undermining its provincial cousins?
items.summary_auto: Avi Lewis, a former television host, journalist and documentarian, promises to make the federal NDP relevant again. But the first question for his leadership is whether he'll do so at the expense of the NDP's provincial wings.

items.title: Céline Dion to return to concert stage with Paris residency this fall
items.summary_auto: It's Céline Dion's 58th birthday, but it's her adoring fans who may be receiving the biggest gift of all — her return to the concert stage.

items.title: Israeli parliament approves the death penalty for Palestinians convicted of murdering Israelis
items.summary_auto: The bill's passage marked the culmination of a years-long push by Israel's far-right to escalate punishment for Palestinians convicted of nationalistic offenses against Israelis.

items.title: Carney defends Canada's approach to forced labour amid fallout from floor-crossing MP's comments
Brain and heart health are tied together. Here are 5 ways to control both
items.summary_auto: Pay attention to the link between heart and brain health, new Canadian recommendations say.

items.title: What impacts your heart health can also raise dementia risk, new Canadian guidelines say
items.summary_auto: Pay attention to the link between heart and brain health, new Canadian recommendations say.

items.title: Train derails in St. Catharines, Ont., CN Railway investigating
items.summary_auto: CN Railway is investigating a train derailment in St. Catharines, Ont. The derailment was reported by police Monday morning, near Glendale Avenue and Keele Street.

items.title: Air Canada employee charged after alleged weed smuggling scheme using passenger luggage tags
items.summary_auto: The RCMP says an Air Canada employee is facing charges after allegedly trying to export more than 60 kilograms of cannabis out of a Toronto airport by using suitcases tagged with the names of two unsuspecting passengers.

items.title: Northern Manitoba, Sask. communities working to resolve cross-border health-care issues
items.summary_auto: People in and around Flin Flon have started a health care committee to improve regional care. Saskatchewan residents use the hospital in the northern Manitoba community, but sometimes have trouble accessing care.

items.title: 3 Indonesian UN peacekeepers killed within 24 hours in south Lebanon
items.summary_auto: The United Nations Interim Force In Lebanon (UNIFIL) said three of its peacekeepers were killed in south Lebanon in less than 24 hours in separate incidents, as peace remains elusive amid a nearly month-long war between Israel and Hezbollah.

items.title: 'MAID is not death on demand': Physicians decry proposed Alberta assistance in dying bill
The humpback whale rescued last week in the shallow Baltic Sea? It's stuck again
items.summary_auto: A humpback whale that was freed after becoming stuck for several days in shallow water at a Baltic Sea resort in Germany was stranded again on Saturday after failing to find its way back to the Atlantic Ocean.

items.title: Thieves steal Renoir, Cézanne, Matisse works in 3-minute Italian museum raid
items.summary_auto: Thieves made off with three paintings by Renoir, Cézanne and Matisse worth millions of euros from a museum near the city of Parma in northern Italy, police said on Monday.

items.title: Air Canada CEO Michael Rousseau to retire later this year following language controversy
Transgender Quebecers face surgery delays, out-of-province patients get faster care
items.summary_auto: GrS Montreal, the sole clinic in Quebec offering fully subsidized gender-affirming surgeries, recently announced that a change in provincial funding will delay wait times — possibly by years — but only for Quebec patients.

items.title: Over 70 killed in South Sudan in dispute over gold mine
items.summary_auto: Gunmen killed more than 70 people in South Sudan over a gold mining row on the outskirts of the capital over the weekend, a police spokesperson confirmed on Monday.

items.title: Sask. could face another wicked fire season as experts call for more prevention
items.summary_auto: Experts say Saskatchewan could face a severe wildfire season similar to last year, driven by dry conditions, low soil moisture and unusually hot weather. Some warn the province isn't prepared to handle what is now the new normal for the season.

items.title: Calgary for-profit plasma clinic rated 'non-compliant' during Health Canada inspection
items.summary_auto: A Calgary private plasma collection centre was designated as “non-compliant” by Health Canada in December for violating rules regulating the country’s blood supply. The revelation is sparking new questions about safety.

items.title: Manitoba denying trans refugee's request for name change because it won't accept her ID
items.summary_auto: A Ukrainian refugee says Manitoba's rejection of her requests to help her remove her deadname from official documents leaves her exposed to transphobia and infringes on her right to express her gender.

items.title: Trump threatens to seize Kharg Island oil terminal, attacking Iran's energy infrastructure
items.summary_auto: U.S. President Donald Trump openly mused about seizing Iran's Kharg Island oil terminal in the Persian Gulf as the United States and Israel kept up their attacks on Monday, even as there were signs of progress in nascent ceasefire talks.

items.title: 'Light also exists': Some Cubans see hope in the darkness of the moment
items.summary_auto: The U.S. oil blockade has pushed Cuba into a third month of energy crisis, deepening daily hardship in a country already strained by crumbling infrastructure. But amid the darkness, some Cubans still see hope for a better future.

items.title: Dad speaks out after teen ordered banned testosterone booster online
items.summary_auto: An Ottawa father says he was shocked to discover that his teenage son had ordered banned pharmaceuticals from the internet, and is warning others about websites that he says are putting kids at risk.

items.title: More than 67,500 Ontario kids waiting for core autism funding as demand grows
items.summary_auto: Despite increases to provincial funding in recent years, the percentage of children with autism registered with the Ontario Autism Program and receiving funding had yet to crack 25 per cent as of early this year — as demand for the funding continues to grow.

items.title: Junos 2026: Drake's surprise appearance, and Tate McRae's absence mark Canada's biggest night in music
items.summary_auto: The 2026 Juno Awards swept through a short list of awards and long list of performances Sunday night, honouring up-and-comers and staples of the Canadian music scene.
